content:
# Allergen Gap Assessment

**Tool ID:** T008-01
**Block:** B008 (Allergen Prevention Programme)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Assessment
**Role:** Allergen Champion
**Time Required:** 30-45 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. It is designed to be picked up and used immediately, no training required.

A structured way to evaluate your entire allergen management system in one sitting. This is not about individual staff performance -- it is about the system. Are your processes, documents, training, and kitchen practices working together to prevent an allergen incident? This assessment finds the gaps before an EHO inspector or, worse, a customer reaction does.

**Built for Allergen Champion working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

RAG-rated assessment tool covering all allergen management areas: matrix accuracy, PPDS labelling, staff knowledge, kitchen practices, and documentation. Identifies immediate gaps and prioritises corrective actions.

This tool supports **Allergen Prevention Programme** (B008) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Allergen Champion (or Manager if no Allergen Champion designated)
**Time Required:** 30-45 minutes for full assessment
**Frequency:** Monthly (minimum); immediately after any menu change, supplier change, or allergen incident

**What You'll Need:**
- This assessment form on clipboard
- Allergen Matrix (T005-01) for cross-reference
- PPDS labels and the PPDS Item Audit (T006-01)
- Access to the kitchen, counter, and all preparation areas
- Access to training records
- 30 minutes of uninterrupted focus (not during service)

**Steps:**
1. Work through each assessment area below systematically
2. Score each item as RED (non-compliant/missing), AMBER (partially compliant/needs improvement), or GREEN (fully compliant)
3. Any RED item requires immediate corrective action before the next service
4. AMBER items must have a corrective action plan within 1 week
5. GREEN items should be maintained -- do not let standards slip
6. Complete the action plan at the bottom
7. Review findings with the Manager and agree priorities

---

## Assessment

### Assessment Information

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Assessor (Allergen Champion) | _____ |
| Date | _____ |
| Assessment type | ☐ Monthly routine / ☐ Post-menu change / ☐ Post-incident / ☐ Post-supplier change |

### Area 1: Allergen Matrix (T005-01)

| # | Criterion | RED | AMBER | GREEN | Evidence / Notes |
|---|----------|-----|-------|-------|-----------------|
| 1.1 | Allergen matrix exists and is current (updated within last 7 days) | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Last update date: _____ |
| 1.2 | Every menu item is listed on the matrix (including specials and seasonal items) | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Missing items: _____ |
| 1.3 | All 14 EU allergens are assessed for every item | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |
| 1.4 | Matrix is verified against actual recipes and supplier spec sheets | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Last verification: _____ |
| 1.5 | Matrix is accessible to all front-of-house staff (posted at till, not filed in office) | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Location: _____ |
| 1.6 | Kitchen copy matches counter copy (single version of truth) | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |

### Area 2: PPDS Labelling (T006-01, T006-03)

| # | Criterion | RED | AMBER | GREEN | Evidence / Notes |
|---|----------|-----|-------|-------|-----------------|
| 2.1 | All PPDS items identified and listed | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Total PPDS items: _____ |
| 2.2 | Every PPDS item has a compliant label (product name, ingredients, allergens bold, business name) | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Non-compliant items: _____ |
| 2.3 | Labels are legible and firmly attached to products | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |
| 2.4 | Labels match actual ingredients currently in use (no outdated labels) | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |
| 2.5 | Process exists for updating labels when recipes or suppliers change | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |

### Area 3: Staff Knowledge and Training

| # | Criterion | RED | AMBER | GREEN | Evidence / Notes |
|---|----------|-----|-------|-------|-----------------|
| 3.1 | All front-of-house staff know how to respond to an allergen enquiry | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Test: ask 2 staff members a question |
| 3.2 | All staff know where to find the Allergen Matrix | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Can they locate it in under 10 seconds? |
| 3.3 | All staff know who to escalate a severe allergy to | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Can they name the person? |
| 3.4 | Allergen dialogue role-play assessments completed for all staff | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Last assessment date: _____ |
| 3.5 | New staff receive allergen training before serving customers solo | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Training records on file: ☐ Yes ☐ No |
| 3.6 | No staff member says "I think it is fine" when asked about allergens | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Observe during service |

### Area 4: Kitchen Practices

| # | Criterion | RED | AMBER | GREEN | Evidence / Notes |
|---|----------|-----|-------|-------|-----------------|
| 4.1 | Separate utensils/boards used for allergen-free preparation when needed | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |
| 4.2 | Surfaces cleaned between preparing items with different allergen profiles | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |
| 4.3 | Allergen-containing ingredients clearly stored and labelled | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | e.g., nut-containing items separate |
| 4.4 | Shared equipment (Vitamix, toastie press) cleaned between uses with different allergens | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |
| 4.5 | Ingredient substitutions trigger immediate allergen review | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Process documented? |

### Area 5: Documentation and Records

| # | Criterion | RED | AMBER | GREEN | Evidence / Notes |
|---|----------|-----|-------|-------|-----------------|
| 5.1 | Supplier specification sheets on file for all bought-in products | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | Missing specs: _____ |
| 5.2 | Allergen Conversation Log (T007-03) in use for severe allergy interactions | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |
| 5.3 | Recipe cards include allergen information | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |
| 5.4 | Allergen management records filed in SFBB diary for EHO evidence | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |
| 5.5 | Written allergen management policy exists and is communicated to all staff | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | |

### Overall Scoring

| Area | Total GREEN | Total AMBER | Total RED | Overall RAG |
|------|-----------|------------|----------|-------------|
| 1. Allergen Matrix | ___/6 | ___/6 | ___/6 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |
| 2. PPDS Labelling | ___/5 | ___/5 | ___/5 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |
| 3. Staff Knowledge | ___/6 | ___/6 | ___/6 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |
| 4. Kitchen Practices | ___/5 | ___/5 | ___/5 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |
| 5. Documentation | ___/5 | ___/5 | ___/5 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |

### Overall Assessment

- Overall competency level: ☐ RED (Critical gaps -- act today) / ☐ AMBER (Gaps identified -- fix within 1 week) / ☐ GREEN (Compliant -- maintain standards)
- Biggest strength: _______________
- Most urgent gap: _______________
- Recommended priority actions: _______________

### Corrective Action Plan

| Priority | Gap Identified | Action Required | Owner | Deadline | Status |
|----------|---------------|-----------------|-------|----------|--------|
| 1 (urgent) | | | | | ☐ Open ☐ Done |
| 2 | | | | | ☐ Open ☐ Done |
| 3 | | | | | ☐ Open ☐ Done |
| 4 | | | | | ☐ Open ☐ Done |

### Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Date |
|------|------|------|
| Allergen Champion (assessor) | _____ | _____ |
| Manager (reviewed and agreed actions) | _____ | _____ |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Assessment Scores Everything as GREEN Without Genuine Verification

**Why it happens:** The Allergen Champion wants to show compliance so ticks GREEN without actually checking. They assume the matrix is up to date without verifying against recipes. They assume staff know the procedure without asking them.
**How to prevent:** For each criterion, note the EVIDENCE in the final column. "Staff know where the matrix is" must be verified by asking 2 staff members to show you -- do not assume. "Matrix is current" must be verified by checking the date stamp. Evidence turns assumptions into facts.
**If it is already happening:** Ask a colleague to shadow the next assessment. A second pair of eyes catches complacency.

### Risk 2: Corrective Actions Identified but Never Completed

**Why it happens:** The assessment identifies 4 gaps. The action plan is written. Then the cafe gets busy and nobody follows through. The same gaps appear in the next assessment.
**How to prevent:** Give every action an owner and a deadline. Review progress weekly at the team meeting. If an action is not done by the deadline, escalate to the Manager. Track completion rates -- if the same gap appears twice, it becomes a priority.
**If it is already happening:** Focus on the single most urgent RED item. Fix that one thing completely. Then move to the next. Momentum builds from completion, not from long lists.

### Risk 3: Assessment Not Done After Menu or Supplier Changes

**Why it happens:** A new seasonal cake is added to the menu. The Kitchen Lead creates the recipe and starts selling it. Nobody triggers an allergen review. The Allergen Matrix is not updated. A customer asks about the new cake and staff cannot answer.
**How to prevent:** Create a rule: any new menu item or supplier change triggers an immediate mini-assessment (Area 1 and Area 2 only, 10 minutes). Post this rule in the kitchen: "New product = allergen check BEFORE it goes on sale."
**If it is already happening:** Audit all items added in the last 3 months. Verify each one is on the matrix with correct allergen data.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** You may not have a designated Allergen Champion. In that case, the Manager conducts this assessment. Consider appointing a team member who shows interest in food safety as your Allergen Champion.
**Chain / Franchise:** Your brand may have corporate allergen audit requirements. Use this tool to supplement and ensure local compliance beyond the corporate checklist.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Simplify Areas 4 and 5 (kitchen practices and documentation). Focus on Areas 1-3 (matrix, labelling, staff knowledge) as your critical priorities.
**Bakery Cafe:** Area 4 (kitchen practices) is especially important for bakeries where flour dust (gluten), eggs, milk, and nuts are handled constantly. Add specific criteria for bakery cross-contact prevention.

**Making it yours:**
- Adjust the criteria to match your specific setup and risk profile
- Add criteria for any allergens that are particularly relevant to your menu
- Consider a digital version that tracks scores over time for trend analysis
- Share results with the whole team -- transparency drives improvement

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T008-02 -- Monthly Allergen Audit Checklist (L2, audit)
- T008-03 -- Allergen Incident Response Protocol (L2, protocol)
- T008-04 -- Allergen Training Competency Record (L2, tracker)
- T008-05 -- Allergen Management Policy Template (L2, template)

**Related blocks:**

**Parent block:** B008 -- Allergen Prevention Programme
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
